action.reasoning: Observation: The model from iter 7 achieved mae_60min of 3.5058, which is worse than the iter 6 model (3.4475). The increased capacity and Weighted MAE loss likely caused overfitting or instability. We have 43 minutes left in the time budget. Analysis: To get the best possible score before time runs out, we should revert to the iter 6 architecture (channels=64, node_dim=40) and standard L1 loss, which proved to be highly effective. We will train for 70 epochs, which will take approximately 38 minutes, fitting safely within our remaining 43-minute budget. We will also revert to using val_mae_avg for model selection, as it provided a more stable signal in iter 6. Decision: Update model.py, train.py, and predict.py to revert to the iter 6 configuration, train for 70 epochs, and submit the final evaluation.
